cond.c(3) Library Functions Manual cond.c(3)

NAME

cond.c - Platform-agnostic condition variable hooks and utilities.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void cond_on_wait (cond_t *cond, mutex_t *mutex, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
Hook called when a thread waits on a condition variable. void cond_on_signal (cond_t *cond)
Hook called when a condition variable is signaled. void cond_on_broadcast (cond_t *cond)
Hook called when a condition variable is broadcast. int cond_format_state (const cond_t *cond, char *buffer, size_t size)
Format condition variable timing and state info into buffer. void cond_log_state (const cond_t *cond, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
Log the state of a condition variable.

Detailed Description

Platform-agnostic condition variable hooks and utilities.

This file provides hook functions that can be called from platform-specific condition variable implementations to record events and debug information. All hooks are debug-only and disabled in release builds.

Definition in file cond.c.

Function Documentation

int cond_format_state (const cond_t * cond, char * buffer, size_t size)

Format condition variable timing and state info into buffer.

Parameters

cond Pointer to the condition variable
buffer Output buffer
size Buffer size

Returns

Number of bytes written

Formats timing (wait/signal/broadcast), waiting threads, and operation counts. Called by cond_log_state() and --sync-state display code.

Definition at line 92 of file cond.c.

92                                                                      {
93   if (!cond || !buffer || size == 0) return 0;
94 
95   int offset = 0;
96   uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
97 
98   char wait_str[64] = "";
99   char signal_str[64] = "";
100   char broadcast_str[64] = "";
101   char waiting_str[64] = "";
102   char count_str[256] = "";
103 
104   if (cond->last_wait_time_ns > 0 && cond->last_wait_time_ns <= now_ns) {
105     char elapsed_str[64];
106     time_pretty(now_ns - cond->last_wait_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
107     snprintf(wait_str, sizeof(wait_str), "wait=%s", elapsed_str);
108   }
109 
110   if (cond->last_signal_time_ns > 0 && cond->last_signal_time_ns <= now_ns) {
111     char elapsed_str[64];
112     time_pretty(now_ns - cond->last_signal_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
113     snprintf(signal_str, sizeof(signal_str), "signal=%s", elapsed_str);
114   }
115 
116   if (cond->last_broadcast_time_ns > 0 && cond->last_broadcast_time_ns <= now_ns) {
117     char elapsed_str[64];
118     time_pretty(now_ns - cond->last_broadcast_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
119     snprintf(broadcast_str, sizeof(broadcast_str), "broadcast=%s", elapsed_str);
120   }
121 
122   uint64_t waiting_count = atomic_load_u64(&cond->waiting_count);
123   if (waiting_count > 0) {
124     snprintf(waiting_str, sizeof(waiting_str), "[WAITING=%lu]", waiting_count);
125   }
126 
127   if (cond->wait_count > 0 || cond->signal_count > 0 || cond->broadcast_count > 0) {
128     snprintf(count_str, sizeof(count_str), "[ops: wait=%lu signal=%lu broadcast=%lu]",
129              cond->wait_count, cond->signal_count, cond->broadcast_count);
130   }
131 
132   offset += snprintf(buffer + offset, size - offset, "%s %s %s %s %s",
133                      wait_str, signal_str, broadcast_str, waiting_str, count_str);
134   return offset;
135 }




References atomic_load_u64(), time_get_ns(), and
    time_pretty().


Referenced by cond_log_state().






void
  cond_log_state (const cond_t * cond, const char * file, int line, const char *
  func)


Log the state of a condition variable.


Parameters


cond Pointer to the condition variable


file Source file of the caller


line Source line of the caller


func Source function of the caller


Formats and logs detailed state information about the condition
    variable.


Definition at line 148 of file cond.c.


148                                                                                       {
149   if (!cond) return;
150   char buf[512];
151   cond_format_state(cond, buf, sizeof(buf));
152   log_msg(LOG_DEBUG, file, line, func, "cond/state %p: %s", (const void *)cond, buf);
153 }






References cond_format_state(), and log_msg().






void
  cond_on_broadcast (cond_t * cond)


Hook called when a condition variable is broadcast.


Parameters


cond Pointer to the condition variable being
  broadcast


Called by platform-specific cond_broadcast() after waking all
    threads. Records timing and broadcasts count.


Definition at line 73 of file cond.c.


73                                      {
74   if (!cond) return;
75   cond->last_broadcast_time_ns = time_get_ns();
76   cond->broadcast_count++;
77   atomic_store_u64(&cond->waiting_count, 0);
78 }






References atomic_store_u64(), and
  time_get_ns().






void
  cond_on_signal (cond_t * cond)


Hook called when a condition variable is signaled.


Parameters


cond Pointer to the condition variable being
  signaled


Called by platform-specific cond_signal() after waking one thread.
    Records timing and decrements waiting count.


Definition at line 55 of file cond.c.


55                                   {
56   if (!cond) return;
57   cond->last_signal_time_ns = time_get_ns();
58   cond->signal_count++;
59   if (atomic_load_u64(&cond->waiting_count) > 0) {
60     atomic_fetch_sub_u64(&cond->waiting_count, 1);
61   }
62 }






References atomic_fetch_sub_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), and time_get_ns().






void
  cond_on_wait (cond_t * cond, mutex_t * mutex, const char * file, int line,
  const char * func)


Hook called when a thread waits on a condition variable.


Parameters


cond Pointer to the condition variable being
  waited on


mutex Pointer to the associated mutex


file Source file of the wait callsite (for deadlock detection)


line Source line of the wait callsite (for deadlock detection)


func Source function of the wait callsite (for deadlock detection)


Called by platform-specific cond_wait() or cond_timedwait() before
    blocking. Records timing, callsite information, and increments wait
  count.


Definition at line 33 of file cond.c.


33                                                                                               {
34   if (!cond) return;
35   cond->last_wait_time_ns = time_get_ns();
36   cond->last_wait_mutex = mutex;
37   cond->last_wait_file = file;
38   cond->last_wait_line = line;
39   cond->last_wait_func = func;
40   asciichat_thread_t current_thread = (asciichat_thread_t)asciichat_thread_current_id();
41   cond->last_waiting_key = asciichat_thread_to_key(current_thread);
42   atomic_fetch_add_u64(&cond->waiting_count, 1);
43   cond->wait_count++;
44 }






References asciichat_thread_current_id(),
    atomic_fetch_add_u64(), mutex, and time_get_ns().
